titleStmt.title: Letter from Howard Kelly to White, 1 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Howard Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Howard Kelly was Commander of the HMS Gloucester during WWI. He would subsequently enjoy an impressive career in the navy including acting as British Naval Mission to Greece. He also became Commander of the 1st Battle Squadron in the Atlantic Fleet in 1923 and commander of the 2nd Cruiser Squadron in 1925.
He went on to be Admiralty representative to the League of Nations in 1927 and commander of the 1st Battle Squadron and second-in-command of the Mediterranean Fleet in 1929. His last appointment was intended to be Commander-in-Chief, China Station in 1931.
Arethusa Leigh-White married Edward Leigh-White in 1904 and played a prominent part in life in the Bantry area throughout her lifetime. She would serve as director of the World Association of Girl Guides and Girl Scouts from 1938-1947. This letter is both personal nature but also details some aspects of life on-board during wartime.
This is a letter from the extensive Bantry Estate Collection, held in the Boole Library of University College Cork. The collection, which covers a period of 300 years, contains the formal records regarding the legal, financial and general administration of this large house and estate in County Cork.

msIdentifier.repository: UCC Library Special Collections and Archives

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
H.M.S. Gloucester Jan 1st 1916 Dear Mrs Leigh-White You are still better known
to me by name, and from
long before George & Madge
had even met you, and
Eva White at Coomhoola is
a first cousin of mine, but
you had always happened to
be away as many my visits to
Bantry. I am afraid I
haven't heard anything for the
<twins except to present their
wings separated. it was really
rather pathetic, one got the
mumps, and now-a-days in
war times any one leaving the
ship for hospital or elsewhere
leaves for good. After the sick
one had left I sent for the
other to tell him to write and
tell his mother I would get
the twins back some how and
her arrival in my cabin in
floods of tears, though I don't
think they bother about each
other when they are together. They are both very good boys
and quite bright and smart in
their minds, and after all the
colour of their minds is more
important than the colourof their
hands and feet, especially in
war times. Owing to our
unfortunate depart in the ship
she will probably be seeing home
again for another fourteen days in
about a weeks time, which
I hope won't be too much for
their family .
I am hoping to spend a night
at at the end of next
week. Yours sincerely Howard Kelly.
